_about.status: v3 — adds the MONTHLY corpus beside the untouched weekly one (migration 010's report_kind). Weekly lines are byte-identical to v2, in the same order, so every deployed index keeps drawing the same line. This file is the corpus of the weekly/monthly report engine ONLY: yearly is a Vimshottari composition over dasha_content, and never flows through here.
_about.axis: weekly: shape (6) x 17 openings | turn (5) x 7 | area.role (18) x 5 | close (6) x 5. monthly: shape (5, WHICH WEEK CARRIES THE MONTH) x 13 openings | turn (4, the month's pivot at week granularity) x 7 | area.role (18) x 5 | close (5) x 5.
_about.counts: weekly: 257 lines. monthly: 65 openings + 28 turns + 90 standings + 25 closes = 208 lines. total 465.
_about.why_monthly_is_not_a_long_weekly: Weekly claims are about DAYS (where the strong days fall in seven). Running that over 30 days would be a longer list, not a different report. Monthly claims are about WEEKS: which week carries the month (shape), whether the month pivots at a week boundary or tilts across its halves (turn), and which area leads/lags/steadies over the whole span. Rejected after measuring 144 real months: week-over-week trajectory (the weekly means are the tara sawtooth aliased through a 7-day window — a trend claim would describe the aliasing, the exact smoothing artefact the weekly taxonomy already rejected) and recurring-weekday patterns (real in 46% of months but 4-5 samples against a 9-day cycle; true this month, false as the generalisation a reader would take).
_about.division_of_labour: WEEKLY OWNS DAYS, MONTHLY OWNS WEEKS — the IDENTITY.md §5 rule one level up. Monthly copy never names a day or a weekday; weekly copy never names the month. Monthly anchors are ISO weeks (the weekly report's own key), so the two reports corroborate instead of repeating. Enforced by tests/test_report_cross_kind.py.
_about.why_13: The monthly cycle to clear is 12 (reports per calendar year), not 52. 13 — the one prime that could NOT work at week scale because it divides 52 — satisfies gcd(13,12)=1 and is the smallest count above 12, so 13 consecutive monthly openings are distinct (outlasting a full year) and an anniversary month repeats an opening only after 13 years (12 ≡ -1 mod 13). 13/7/5 are pairwise coprime and each coprime with 12: full skeleton recurs every lcm = 455 months (~38 years).
_about.thresholds: Derived from 144 real months x 6 natals, NOT copied from weekly: weekly-mean spread inside a month measured 1.4-30.3 (median 13.7) against the 48-70 daily spread inside a week. LEVEL_SPREAD=6, TWIN_MARGIN=2, HALF_MARGIN=4. Measured shape distribution: core 62%, closing 15%, opening 11%, twin 7%, level 4% — all five classes reachable, unlike weekly's dead 'even'. Turns: hinge 33%, steady 25%, lifts 23%, settles 19%.

openings: This week is level. Nothing distinguishes one day from another, which makes it excellent for anything requiring consistency.
An even seven days. No day to aim at and none to avoid, so the schedule can be whatever you decide.
It holds the same note throughout. Routine tasks, long projects and anything needing repetition all suit this shape.
There is no rhythm here worth planning around. That is not emptiness; it is the absence of interference.
A plain week. Nothing argues with you in either direction, so your own discipline sets the pace.
Every day sits at one level. Weeks like this are where backlogs actually get cleared.
No standout days in either direction. The differences are smaller than your own mood will be.
It runs flat. Pick the project that benefits from a long uninterrupted run and give it all seven days.
This one does not move much. Treat the whole as a single block rather than as parts.
Uniform throughout. What you get out of it depends almost entirely on what you put in.
A quiet, unvarying week. Nothing will carry you and nothing will obstruct you.
Seven days of the same weather. Choose what deserves a steady run and give it the whole stretch.
Nothing here rewards timing. Effort is the only variable left, which at least makes the arithmetic simple.
The week neither helps nor hinders. Progress will look exactly like whatever you repeated.
No peaks to catch and no dips to dodge. Set a pace you can hold for seven days and hold it.
Level from Monday to Sunday. If you have been waiting for a neutral week to begin a routine, this is it.
One flat line all the way across. Judge your days by what got finished, because the sky will not rank them.
Your strongest days land at the front. Whatever needs sharp judgement should be scheduled before midweek, not after.
The week gives most of what it has early. Treat the opening as prime time rather than as a warm-up.
Front-loaded. The two highest days arrive before most people have finished planning, so do your planning on Sunday.
The strong days cluster at the start. That is convenient if you act on it and wasteful if you do not.
This week opens at its best. Nothing later matches those first days, so spend them rather than saving them.
The high days come early. Anything waiting for the right moment has its moment almost immediately.
The first half holds most of it. Book the hard conversations there and let the rest be admin.
Early days carry this one. By the back half you will be working on what you set up, not setting up anything new.
The opening days are the ones that count. Judge the week by what you do in them.
Strength arrives immediately and does not wait around. Move the important thing forward in your calendar.
The best of this week is at the beginning. Read that as an instruction about scheduling, not about luck.
The week pays out in advance. Take the difficult conversation early and coast on the result.
Monday and Tuesday do the heavy lifting here. Arrive with the list already written.
What this week gives, it gives first. Waiting costs more than usual, so decide quickly and act on it.
The opening carries the value. Spend it on decisions, because the later days suit routine better.
Best days first, quieter days after. Sequence the hard work accordingly and the whole week feels generous.
By Wednesday the best of it has been given. Whatever needs sharp judgement should not sit late in the calendar.
Your strongest days are at the end. Push what matters later rather than forcing it early.
The week saves its strongest for last. The opening days are for preparation and they are genuinely enough for that.
Back-loaded. Whatever feels heavy at the start gets noticeably lighter once the strong days arrive.
The high days cluster near the close. Protect them — do not let routine fill the end of your week.
Most of the strength sits in the second half. Move deadlines back where you honestly can.
This one finishes better than it starts. Do not judge it on Monday.
The capable days come late. Anything requiring sharp judgement belongs at the far end.
Strength arrives toward the finish. Use the opening to clear the ground so the strong days are free.
The closing days are the ones that count. Everything before them is legitimately preparation.
This week's peak is at the end. Arrange things so you are not too tired to use it.
Later is stronger. Every task you can honestly defer gets better support when you defer it.
The capable days hold back until the finish. Clear the ground beforehand so they arrive unencumbered.
A slow open and a sound finish. Put the decisions late and the paperwork early.
What matters lands late this week. Resist forcing it ahead of time; the support is simply not there yet.
The far half holds the good days. Aim anything that needs judgement there and keep the opening light.
The last days are the capable ones. Until they arrive, preparation is the honest work.
Nothing about a slow opening is a verdict. The days that decide this week all sit in its second half.
This week is strong at both ends and thin through the middle. Book the demanding things either side of the centre.
Two good runs with a gap between them. The gap is short and it does not need managing.
The strength sits at the edges. Midweek is for maintenance, and there is no shame in planning it that way.
Both ends are better than the centre. Plan two pushes rather than one long one.
You get a strong opening and a strong close. What sits between them is quieter than either.
The middle is the soft spot. Schedule around it and this reads as two solid runs rather than one uneven week.
Energy comes in two blocks. Treat them as separate weeks and the middle stops being a problem.
Strong, thin, strong. Knowing the order in advance is most of what you need.
The centre asks least of you. Let it, and put the real effort on either side.
This one has a dip in the middle with strength around it. The dip is bounded, which is the useful bit.
The edges carry this week. Midweek is for whatever tolerates a slower pace.
Strong edges and a quiet middle. Two short campaigns with a rest between them is the natural plan.
The week works in bookends. Open hard, close hard, and let midweek be deliberately unambitious.
Patience is for the days between the pushes. Both ends will repay whatever the centre defers.
A lull sits between two able runs. Schedule the lull on purpose and it stops being one.
Two working stretches with a valley in the middle. Neither is long, so neither should be wasted.
Begin firmly, ease off, finish firmly. The soft centre is part of the design, not a failure of it.
Your strongest days sit in the middle. Aim the thing that matters at midweek rather than at either edge.
The centre carries this one. Both ends are ordinary; what sits between them is not.
Midweek is where the strength is. Use the opening to prepare and the close to consolidate.
The high days are central. That gives you a run-up and a wind-down, which is the most workable arrangement there is.
Neither end of this week tells you much. The middle is where it actually happens.
Strength gathers toward the centre. Book the demanding conversation for midweek and it will go better.
Midweek holds the peak. Do not spend yourself on the opening trying to get ahead.
This one peaks between its ends. Approach that deliberately rather than arriving already tired.
The centre is the strong stretch. Front and back are for setup and tidying respectively.
Midweek does the heavy lifting. Everything else is scaffolding around it.
The best days are neither first nor last. Plan around the middle.
Wednesday to Friday is where the capacity sits. Treat the opening as a ramp and the close as a landing.
This week bulges in the middle. Put the one thing that matters most exactly there.
The edges are ordinary and the centre is not. A push at the halfway mark will get further than an early one.
Capacity peaks midweek and tapers on both sides. Match the workload to that outline and nothing is wasted.
The high days sit shoulder to shoulder in the centre. One sustained effort there beats two half-efforts at the edges.
Open light, peak in the middle, land soft. Weeks built this way reward the patient opener.
The strong days are scattered rather than clustered. Check each morning instead of planning a run.
Capable and thin days alternate. That is workable, but it rewards a flexible schedule over a fixed one.
This week has no block of strength — it comes a day at a time, with gaps between.
Strength arrives in single days rather than runs. Keep the demanding tasks short enough to fit one.
The good days do not sit together. Pair each with a lighter one after it and the week runs evenly.
It alternates. Expect to feel capable and then ordinary in quick succession, and read nothing into either.
No two strong days in a row. Plan the important things one at a time rather than in a push.
The week comes in single high days. That suits errands and decisions better than long projects.
Strength is distributed rather than concentrated. There is no standout run here, only standout days.
The rhythm is uneven. Take the strong days as they come and keep everything else light.
This one gives and withholds by turns. Short commitments will serve you better than long ones.
The strength comes interleaved with ordinary days. Match each task to the day it deserves, morning by morning.
No run of good days this week, only good days singly. Size the work so one day can complete it.
The week deals its cards one at a time. Treat each one as a separate opportunity rather than the start of something.
Good days arrive without warning and leave the same way. A loose plan will outperform a rigid one.
Nothing clusters this week. Alternate the demanding work with the routine and the rhythm takes care of itself.
Expect single good days with plain ones between. Keep commitments day-sized and the unevenness never costs you.

lines: The strongest hours arrive almost immediately, so the decision worth making is what you point them at.
Your high lands near the start, which gives you everything after it to build on.
The turn happens early. Past it the week is not weaker so much as differently shaped.
Peak hours come before most people have settled in. Be ready rather than warmed up.
It opens near its best. Waiting for a better moment means waiting past it.
The high sits at the front. Everything later is downstream of what you do there.
Early is when this week is at its most capable. Treat the first hours as the main event.
The pivot is in the middle. What happens there sets the tone for everything after.
Your strongest hours sit at the centre, with room on both sides to prepare and recover.
The turn comes midweek. Approach it deliberately rather than arriving at it already spent.
The middle carries the high. Book the thing you have been putting off against it.
Conditions top out in the centre. Neither end is where the leverage is.
The hinge is midweek. Before it is preparation; after it is consequence.
Peak sits between the ends. That is the most workable position it can take.
The strongest conditions arrive at the end. Everything before them is legitimately preparation.
Your high is late, so resist judging the week before it has finished.
The turn comes near the close. Save what needs strength for it rather than spending early.
Everything peaks in the final hours. That is worth protecting against fatigue.
The strongest hours are the last ones. Arrange things so you can still use them.
Late is when this week is at its best. Do not fill the ending with tidying.
The high lands at the finish. Whatever you have been building meets its best moment there.
There is no pivot to plan around. That is genuinely useful information rather than a gap.
Nothing turns. The conditions you get on the first morning are the conditions you get throughout.
No single moment carries more weight than another. Your schedule can ignore the sky entirely.
There is no best hour to wait for. Start when you are ready and the week will cooperate.
It does not hinge anywhere. Consistency, not timing, is what will move things.
No turn, no peak, no dip. Pick your own moment and it will be as good as any other.
Nothing asks for particular timing. That freedom is the shape.
Your best and worst days are next to each other. The drop is real but it is one day wide.
The high and the low sit back to back. Do not read the weaker one as a trend.
There is a sharp change between two consecutive days. Knowing which order they come in is most of the advantage.
The strongest and thinnest days are neighbours. Plan nothing important across both.
You will go from capable to ordinary overnight, or the reverse. Neither says anything about the week as a whole.
Two adjacent days differ more than the rest combined. Let the weaker one be genuinely light.
The swing between consecutive days is the sharpest thing here. Expect it and it costs nothing.
Love carries the week. Conversations land where you aim them, so say the thing you have been circling.
Closeness comes easily. It is a good week to ask for what you actually want from someone.
The people who matter are reachable. Warmth carries further than usual, and reaching out costs you very little.
Relationships lead. Time spent with someone is worth more now than the same time spent alone.
Your closest connections are where the generosity is. Use it on the relationship you have been neglecting.
Love asks for patience. Nothing is wrong, but conversations need more room than you would normally give them.
Closeness moves slowly now. Let misreadings pass rather than correcting them, and they will settle by themselves.
Relationships want less pressure. A postponed conversation is not an avoided one.
The people around you are harder to read. Ask rather than assume, and expect the answer to take a while.
Connection runs thin. Keep expectations small and you will not be disappointed.
Love is reliable throughout. Whatever else moves, the people close to you stay where you left them.
Relationships hold level. That makes them the right place to rest when everything else is uneven.
Closeness neither peaks nor dips. It is the fixed point you can plan around.
Your connections are the constant here. Nothing asks anything unusual of them.
The people close to you do not shift. Lean there when everything else moves.
Money decisions resolve cleanly. Comparisons make sense and the sensible option is visibly the sensible one.
Your accounts are unusually legible. Good timing for the purchase you researched, not the one you just thought of.
Asks land well. If there is a rate to negotiate or a bill to query, do it now.
Spending decisions come easy — not because more is arriving, but because you can see the trade-offs properly.
Money is where the clarity sits. Review the subscriptions, settle the dues, sign what has been waiting.
Money wants checking rather than committing. Hold the big swing and the small ones will still be fine.
Decisions about spending are murkier than usual. A second opinion is worth more than your first instinct.
Nothing about the accounts is urgent. Routine payments are safe; anything requiring a signature can wait.
Comparisons will not resolve cleanly. Sleep on the ones that matter and revisit them later.
Money asks for the slower hand. Reviewing where it went beats deciding where it goes next.
Money holds level. No decision demands anything of you, which is a reasonable argument for simply not spending.
The accounts stay flat. Neither pressure nor opportunity, so the routine can just run.
Spending sits exactly where it did. What worked before works now, unchanged.
Money neither helps nor hinders. Set it aside and give the attention to whatever is actually moving.
Your finances are quiet. Nothing here needs a decision from you.
Work is where the most is given. Push the thing that has been waiting for a good moment — this is it.
Your professional standing rises. Visibility comes cheap, so let the right people see what you ship.
Career leads. Ship what is nearly finished rather than starting something that will not land in time.
The workplace cooperates. Ask for the thing you need from the person who can grant it.
Work runs ahead of everything else. Take on the piece slightly beyond you; conditions will cover the gap.
Work wants consolidation rather than expansion. Finish before you start.
Professionally, everything moves at half pace. Deliver what is promised and resist adding to the pile.
Career is what resists. Nothing is going wrong; it simply does not reward pushing.
The workplace is harder going. Keep commitments modest and they will all be met.
The job asks for less ambition and more follow-through. That trade is worth making.
Work holds its level. The same effort produces the same result on any morning.
Your professional life stays exactly level. Predictability suits long tasks, so give it one.
Career neither climbs nor slips. Use the flatness for anything needing uninterrupted attention.
The workplace is unremarkable, which is the ideal condition for a backlog.
Your standing stays where it is. Nothing will move it for you, and nothing will move against you.
Thinking is sharp. The problem you have been avoiding because it is complicated is the one to open.
Your attention is worth more than usual. Give it to something deserving the quality it has right now.
Clarity leads. Decisions sitting unresolved will resolve if you sit with them properly.
The mind runs clean. Reading, planning and anything requiring sustained thought all go well.
Focus is available. Protect a long block for the task only good concentration can finish.
Thinking comes slower. Write decisions down rather than trusting you will hold them.
Attention scatters more easily. Shorten the blocks and the work still gets done.
Clarity comes at a cost. Reduce what you are holding at once and the remainder gets sharper.
The mind resists complexity. Save the intricate problem for a better week.
Concentration runs thin. Single tasks, one at a time, with the notifications off.
Thinking holds level — neither brilliant nor foggy, but reliably adequate, which is what most days need.
Attention is the constant. Whatever you could concentrate on before, you can concentrate on now.
The mind stays even. Consistency here is worth more than a peak you have to catch.
Clarity neither improves nor slips. Plan on ordinary thinking and you will not be caught out.
Your focus is dependable throughout. Build the schedule on it.
The body is willing. Good timing to restart something physical you let lapse.
Energy in the body runs high. Movement will feel like less effort than it did recently.
Health leads. Sleep, food and exercise all cooperate, so the habit you want to build has room.
Your physical condition is where the surplus is. Spend some of it rather than banking all of it.
The body cooperates. Push the pace slightly and it will hold.
The body wants rest. That is a scheduling instruction rather than a warning.
Physical energy is genuinely lower. Lighter sessions, earlier nights, and everything goes fine.
Health asks for maintenance rather than gains. Hold the routine and do not add to it.
The body is slower to recover. Build in the gap rather than pushing through it.
Rest is the productive choice. Treat sleep as the task, not the leftover.
The body holds level. No surge and no slump, so the routine you have will simply keep working.
Physical condition is the constant. Nothing here needs adjusting.
Health neither improves nor demands. Keep doing what you were doing.
Your body is entirely predictable. That makes it an easy week for building a habit.
Physical energy stays flat. Consistency, not intensity, is what gets rewarded.
Your mood runs generous. Whatever else happens, how you feel about it stays warm.
Inner weather is bright. Use the buoyancy on something that usually feels heavy.
Mood leads. Ideal timing for the thing requiring you to be in a settled state before you start.
You will feel more like yourself than usual. Spend it on people or work that benefit from that.
The emotional weather is the surplus. It will carry the parts that are thinner.
Mood asks the most of you. Lower what you expect of yourself and everything improves immediately.
Inner weather is unsettled. Do not make permanent decisions from a temporary state.
How you feel will lag behind how things actually are. Check the facts before trusting the feeling.
The emotional weather runs heavier. Fewer commitments, more room, and it passes.
Mood lags behind everything else. Be as patient with yourself as you would be with anyone else.
Mood holds level — neither high nor low, which makes it a reliable base for everything else.
Inner weather is the constant. Nothing will swing how you feel.
How you feel stays even. That evenness is worth using on something needing a calm hand.
The emotional weather is unchanging. Plan on it and you will be right.
Your mood is the fixed point. Let the rest move around it.
A run like this is measured by what you put into it. Look at the output, not the sky.
Close by acknowledging the volume. Level weeks clear more than dramatic ones and get no credit.
Nothing about the rhythm decided this. Whatever happened, you did it.
Finish and start the next one the same way. Continuity is this shape's only real advantage.
End with the routine intact. That is the entire point of seven days that do not move.
Carry the early gains forward. What worked in the first days is what to repeat.
Bank what the strong days produced and do not measure everything by a quiet ending.
Close by tidying rather than starting. New things belong on the other side of Sunday.
The tail is for consolidation. Leave it consolidated and Monday opens clean.
Stop before you are forced to. Ending deliberately beats ending exhausted.
End strong and leave it strong. Monday starts from where this finished.
The gain is real — write down what changed so you can repeat it.
What you built in the last days compounds. Do not undo it with a slow restart.
Finish while the good days are still with you, then stop. The stopping matters too.
Close on the upswing and let the slow opening be forgotten.
You end where you started, which after a thin middle is a genuinely good outcome.
Two good runs is more than most weeks give. Count them separately.
The recovery after the centre is the story. Note that it happened without intervention.
Finish knowing the soft patch was bounded. That is worth remembering next time one arrives.
Judge this week from its edges, not from what sat between them.
Come down deliberately. The descent is part of the rhythm and it deserves a plan.
Consolidate after the strong days rather than chasing them. What midweek gained needs securing.
The peak days have passed by the close — that is expected, not a loss.
Use the tail to finish what the centre started. Nothing new belongs there.
End by writing down what the peak produced. It will not seem obvious later.
Do not draw conclusions from a week this uneven. Wait for a calmer one to decide anything permanent.
Close by writing down what actually happened. The impression will be less accurate than the record.
Let it end without a verdict. Alternating weeks resist summary, and forcing one misleads.
Finish by simplifying. Fewer open threads is the right outcome here.
End with slack still in the schedule. That is the win, not any particular achievement.

openings: No week of this month stands above the rest. Progress here is bought by showing up evenly, not by timing anything.
This month runs level across its weeks. Pick the tasks that reward repetition and let them compound quietly.
The month has no single strong week — its strength is spread thin and wide. Routine will beat inspiration here.
A flat month, measured week against week. Treat that as permission to build habits rather than chase moments.
Every week of this month scores within a narrow band. Nothing needs to be saved for later; nothing needs rescuing either.
The weeks of this month are nearly interchangeable. Schedule by convenience, because the sky offers no better argument.
Expect a month without peaks. What you get instead is dependable footing — the kind long projects are built on.
This is a maintenance month. No week will carry you, and no week will drop you, so the pace is yours to set.
Week by week, the month holds one steady line. Momentum will have to come from you rather than from the calendar.
The month divides into equal weeks with little between them. Use that evenness to clear the backlog no dramatic week ever allows.
Measured across its weeks, this month barely moves. Commit to one slow thing and let the flatness do the carrying.
There is no carrier week this month — the load spreads itself. Plans succeed here on persistence, not placement.
A level month, week after week. If something matters, start it now; waiting for a stronger week will not pay this time.
Two separate weeks carry this month, with a gap between them. Plan two pushes and treat the space between as recovery.
The month's power splits between two non-adjacent weeks. Give each its own goal instead of stretching one goal over both.
This month peaks twice, in weeks that do not touch. What the earlier push leaves unfinished, park it cleanly for the later one.
A twin-peaked month: two strong weeks separated by quieter ground. Book the demanding pieces into those two and defend the rest.
Two weeks stand out this month, and they are not neighbours. Let the lull between them be a feature, not a failure.
The month offers you two distinct windows of strength. Decide now which projects belong to which, and hold the split.
Strength arrives twice this month, with a rest written between. Whatever needs two attempts — a pitch, a repair — this is its month.
This month runs high, low, high across its weeks. Ride the opening crest, then bank what you learned for the second pass.
Count on two carrying weeks this month, spaced apart. Split the big task at its natural seam and assign one half to each.
The month holds a pair of strong weeks with slack between them. Slack is where the details get fixed — use it that way.
Not one peak week but two this month, separated cleanly. The second is a genuine second chance; write it into the plan.
Two well-spaced strong weeks shape this month. Aim the launch at one and the follow-through at the other.
The month's two best weeks stand apart with quieter ground between. Push in those, consolidate between them, and nothing gets wasted.
No other week this month matches the one it starts with. Whatever needs momentum, give it that head start.
This month is front-loaded: its opening week carries the most. Decisions made early ride better ground than decisions delayed.
The month starts at its peak and eases from there. Spend the opening week on what genuinely matters most.
Strength sits at the top of this month. Begin hard, because what follows is real but gentler footing.
The opening week outweighs the rest of the month. Book the launch, the ask, the first move into it deliberately.
This month leads with its best week. Anything that benefits from a confident start — start it now, not later.
Early strength defines this month; the first week carries more than any other. Load it accordingly and lighten the rest.
The month's engine is its first week. Use it to set direction, and the quieter weeks will keep the course you chose.
What this month gives, it gives early. Claim the opening week for the work you have been circling for a while.
A front-carried month: the opening week holds the high ground. Momentum built there will coast further than momentum forced later.
The strongest footing this month is its very first week. If a plan needs a running start, this is where it lives.
Open strong: this month rewards early commitment and gives the first week its best numbers. Later weeks maintain rather than climb.
The month crests immediately. Take the opening week seriously — its advantage is the largest single edge these weeks will offer.
This month builds toward its final week. Point the important effort there and use the approach to prepare, not to force.
The strongest week arrives last this month. Anything you can schedule, slide it late and arrive at it ready.
A late-peaking month: the closing week carries the most. Patience up front is strategy, not delay.
The month saves its best for the end. Draft, gather, and rehearse through the middle so the final week meets you prepared.
Strength accumulates across this month and lands in the last week. Build toward it the way you would build toward an exam.
The closing week is this month's high ground. Deadlines that can move should move toward it, not away.
This month ends stronger than it begins. Let the early weeks be groundwork and keep the finish for what counts.
What this month is building becomes visible only at its close. Hold your nerve through the ordinary weeks in between.
The month's final week outweighs the rest. Aim the decision, the launch, the difficult conversation at that late window.
Momentum gathers late this month. The last week rewards whatever the earlier weeks were honest enough to prepare.
A month that finishes at its peak. Work backwards from the closing week and give every earlier week one supporting job.
This month's peak sits at its far end. Resist judging the whole by a modest start.
Late strength shapes this month: its closing week is the one to protect. Guard it from clutter before the clutter arrives.
The heart of this month is a mid-month week. Clear the approach to it, and let the ends stay light.
This month carries its weight in the middle. Warm up through the opening weeks, peak deliberately, then wind down without guilt.
One week in the month's interior outshines the rest. Put the heaviest lifting there and keep the edges for setup and repair.
The month peaks in its middle weeks, not at either edge. Treat the start as a ramp and the end as a landing.
Mid-month is where this one earns its keep. Whatever must go right, position it inside that central week.
A centre-carried month: the strong week sits in the interior. Protect it from obligations that could live at the edges.
This month is strongest at its mid-point. Neither the start nor the finish is the story — the middle is.
The working heart of this month sits mid-way through. Schedule the make-or-break effort there and let the rest orbit it.
Middle weeks carry this month. If something needs both preparation and follow-up, it could not be better placed.
The month's strongest stretch lies between its edges. Use the opening weeks to load and the final ones to unload.
Strength pools in the middle of this month. Arrive at it with the groundwork finished, not with the groundwork in hand.
This month is built like an arch: the load-bearing week stands mid-span. Lean on it, and keep the ends honest.
Interior strength defines the month. The week that matters is neither first nor last, so pace the edges accordingly.

lines: The month's later half runs stronger than its start. What stalls early is not failing — it is early.
Numbers favour the back half of this month. Move what can move; anything immovable, resource it better up front.
This month lifts after its mid-point. Hold steady through the thinner opening and collect the difference later.
The far half of the month carries more than the near half. Budget effort like money: spend where the return is.
Expect the month to strengthen past its middle. Early friction is the terrain, not a verdict on the plan.
The mid-month line divides thinner from stronger ground here. Cross it before judging how the whole is going.
Most of this month's strength sits past its halfway mark. Choose patience early and ambition late, in that order.
The month's front half offers the better footing. Bank the important wins soon, before the pace softens.
This month spends its strength early and settles after. Front-load ruthlessly; the later weeks are for landing, not launching.
The near half of the month outweighs the far half. What is postponed past the middle inherits thinner footing.
Strength favours the opening half of this month. Take the swing while the footing is at its best.
Past mid-month, the numbers ease off. Nothing collapses — but plans made for the second half should be modest ones.
The month quiets in its second half. Read that as a scheduled exhale and put the pushing before the pause.
Early is the operative word this month: the first half simply scores better. Late starts pay a real, measurable tax.
This month's best and worst weeks sit side by side. Expect the change of gear and refuse to read it as a trend.
The month pivots hard at one week boundary — strongest week directly against thinnest. Plan that handover consciously.
One turn defines this month: its peak week and its trough week are neighbours. Keep commitments flexible around that seam.
The month swings from one extreme week straight into the other. Whatever spans both, split it at the join.
There is a cliff-edge in this month: the strongest and weakest weeks touch. Do not sign anything that assumes the pace will hold.
This month turns sharply once — its high and low weeks sit adjacent. Let the strong one absorb what the weak one cannot.
Expect one abrupt shift mid-stream this month. Peak week and thin week share a border; neither lasts, so plan for the average and mine the extremes.
Neither half of this month outweighs the other. Timing games buy little here — sequencing is a matter of taste.
The month balances: its halves score within a whisker. Order the tasks by logic, not by omens.
No meaningful tilt divides this month's halves. Whatever rhythm you set is the rhythm it will keep.
This month holds its line from end to end. Consistency in, consistency out — the returns match the effort unusually directly.
The halves of this month are twins in the numbers. Split long work anywhere; the seam will not show.
Level halves make this a month without a timing gradient. Choose sequence by dependency and let the sky sit this one out.
The month neither lifts nor settles — its halves pull equal weight. Steadiness like this is rare; use it for the tedious thing.
Love holds the top of the table for the whole month. Relationships repay attention at a rate nothing else here matches.
Across these weeks, love is the strongest suit you have. Long-postponed conversations land softer in a month like this.
The month's most reliable returns are relational. Invest in the people, and the rest of the ledger benefits sideways.
Love leads this month from start to finish. Make the standing arrangement, plan the visit, repair the drifted friendship.
For a full month, closeness costs less effort than usual. That is a window — treat warmth as the month's main project.
Love runs at the back of the field this month. Expect more friction than usual and price it in before it stings.
Relationships ask for more than they return through these weeks. Give the maintenance without keeping score for one month.
The month is thin on romantic ease. Lower the stakes of every conversation you can, and postpone the ones you cannot.
Love is the month's weak flank. Guard it with patience rather than grand gestures — the footing does not support grand gestures.
For the whole month, closeness comes harder. Assume good intent twice as often as feels natural, and revisit anything said in haste.
Love is the month's constant: not its highest line, but its least variable. Lean on the people who are simply there.
Whatever else swings, the bond keeps its footing from one end of the month to the other. Let it be the fixed point the plans hinge on.
The dependable line this month is love. It will not spike — it will not dip either, and that is worth more.
Affection neither surges nor stalls this month; it idles reliably. A good month to formalise what already works.
Love supplies the month's ballast. When the louder areas wobble, the steadying influence is sitting across the table from you.
Money is the month's strongest column. Reviews, renewals, and renegotiations all read clearer while it lasts.
Across the whole month, financial questions resolve faster than anything else. Queue them up and clear the list.
The ledger leads this month. If a budget needs rebuilding or a rate needs challenging, the standing is on your side.
Money holds top place for weeks on end here. The researched purchase, the overdue invoice, the pension form — clear them in this window.
Financial clarity is the month's headline. Decisions about spending made in these weeks will still look sensible from a distance.
Money trails the field this month. Batch the financial admin, do it once with full attention, and expect no favours from it.
The month is weakest where the accounts are concerned. Defer discretionary commitments and let the essential ones be boring.
In these weeks, money questions run heavier than they should. Double-check sums you would normally trust on sight.
Finance lags all month. That argues for holding patterns — renew, maintain, postpone — rather than new positions.
The month gives money matters its thinnest footing. Keep buffers fatter than usual and treat surprises as scheduled.
Money is the month's flattest line — no spikes in either direction. Budget once, then stop re-deciding it every week.
The accounts hold steady while everything else moves. Use that stability as the base layer of every plan.
Financially, the month idles at its usual level. Predictability is the gift here: automate what recurs and look away.
Money neither leads nor drags this month — it simply holds. A holding month is the right month for tidying the structure.
The steadiest numbers this month are the financial ones. If a plan needs one dependable pillar, build on that.
Career carries the month. Visibility, applications, and hard asks all benefit from a full month of favourable standing.
Professional life is where the month keeps its reserves. Take on the project with your name on it while this window holds.
Professionally, these weeks outperform everything else on the board. Move the ambition forward a square or two.
The month backs your working life above all. Ship the thing, raise the hand, book the conversation with the boss.
Career questions get the month's best ground. What has waited for the right moment stops waiting now.
Career sits lowest in the month's standings. Keep delivery boring and reliable, and let ambition wait until the terrain improves.
Work runs uphill for these weeks. Trim promises to what is certain, then beat the trimmed promise quietly.
The month underfunds professional pushes. It is a season for maintenance: clear backlogs, document, sharpen tools.
Professionally, the month asks more than it gives. Decline gracefully where you can and protect the core commitments.
Career is the month's weak column. Avoid staking reputation on outcomes that depend on momentum you do not currently have.
Work is the month's most stable quantity — unspectacular, dependable, and immune to the swings elsewhere. Let it fund the rest.
Career neither shines nor slips this month; it holds. Holding is exactly what a long game needs from a month.
The professional line stays flat all month, and flat is useful: plan around work as the one variable that will not move.
Professionally the month is calm water. Use the calm to fix processes that break in busier seasons.
Career supplies the month's even keel. When choosing where to spend attention, count on work needing the least.
The mind has the month's best numbers. Study, planning, and anything intricate will go further per hour than usual.
Mental clarity leads these weeks. Draft the document, untangle the problem, learn the tool — thinking is cheap this month.
The month favours concentration above everything else it offers. Guard blocks of quiet and spend them on the hardest material.
Focus arrives more easily this month than it has in a while. Aim it at the problems only you can solve.
This is a thinking month above all. Decisions built on analysis now will outlast decisions built on urgency later.
Focus is the month's scarcest resource. Shorten the units of effort and let structure do what concentration cannot.
The mind runs foggy for much of the month. Write everything down; the notes will be sharper than the memory.
Mental sharpness lags all month. Postpone intricate commitments where possible, and check the fine print twice where not.
Deep effort costs double this month. Buy progress with checklists, templates and rituals rather than raw willpower.
The month taxes concentration. Choose fewer open loops — every parked project quietly bills the same account.
Concentration holds a modest, steady line through the month. It will not peak, but it will not desert you either.
The mind is the month's metronome — an even tick you can schedule against while louder areas swing.
Focus stays level across the weeks: no brilliance, no fog. Consistent hours will out-earn heroic ones this month.
Mentally the month runs flat and reliable. Perfect conditions for the long, unglamorous middle of a big project.
The steady quantity this month is attention. Ration it deliberately and it covers everything that matters.
Health leads the month's table. The body recovers faster and complains less — the right time to raise the baseline.
The body is the month's sturdiest asset. Book the check-up, restart the routine, sleep like it is a project.
Physically, these weeks are the best on offer. Habits started now get a tailwind that mid-slump starts never enjoy.
The month puts health at the head of the queue. Follow-through, the usual missing ingredient, is briefly in supply — spend it on the body.
Vitality outpaces every other area this month. Physical goals set here start with the wind behind them.
Health brings up the rear this month. Sleep, food and pacing need managing on purpose, not by default.
The body runs below its average for most of the month. Cut the heroics, keep the basics, and count that as discipline.
Physically the month is thin. Schedule recovery the way you schedule appointments — as fixed, not as leftovers.

Health is the month's level line — no surges, no crashes. The routine you keep is the result you get.
The body holds steady while the month moves around it. Reliable energy in modest amounts: budget it, and it suffices.
Physically, the month is even-tempered. Perfect conditions for building a habit too small to fail.
Health supplies the month's most predictable numbers. Let training plans assume an average — they will get exactly that.
No area is calmer than the body this month. Calm is a foundation; put something worthwhile on top of it.

Emotional weather is the kindest part of this month. Hard conversations, fresh starts and bold asks all borrow from it.
Spirits lead the month. Use the buoyancy to bank goodwill: apologise, thank, invite, host.
Mood runs ahead of circumstance this month. Enjoy the gap — and make one or two decisions inside it.
The month's strongest current is emotional. Let it carry the projects that stall when enthusiasm is rationed.
Mood is the month's low mark. Feelings will run behind facts, so audit the facts before believing the feelings.
The emotional baseline sags across these weeks. Plan gentler slopes: fewer stakes per conversation, more slack per plan.
Spirits lag the month. Do not renegotiate your life from inside a dip — log the thought and revisit it on higher ground.
Mood underperforms everything else on the month's board. Externalise it: lists, walks, company. Interiors amplify it.
The month runs emotionally heavier than its numbers justify. Treat low mood as weather to dress for, not truth to obey.
Mood is the month's flattest, friendliest line. Emotional predictability like this is rare — spend it on things that usually scare you.
The emotional keel stays even all month. Decisions made here inherit that evenness; it is a good month to choose.
Spirits hold their level across the weeks. No euphoria to overpromise on, no trough to dig out of — just usable calm.
Mood neither lifts nor drops this month; it accompanies. Stability of feeling is quiet capital — invest it in a hard task.
The steadiest line on the month's board is mood. When plans wobble, the wobble will not be coming from inside.
Close the month by measuring output, not excitement. A level month that shipped something beats a dramatic one that talked about it.
Carry forward the routines that survived a month with no help from the sky. Those are the real ones.
Write down, at the close, what steady effort actually produced. The total will be larger than the month felt.
A month like this proves progress does not need peaks. Keep whatever cadence you found — it transfers.
Leave the month with your systems intact and your expectations calibrated. Flat ground, honestly walked, still covers distance.
Looking back, judge the month by both pushes, not the gap between them. The quiet middle was load-bearing.
Close out by pairing the two strong weeks in memory: what the first started, the second should have moved. Note what neither touched.
The month ends as it ran — in two chapters. File each separately and resist averaging them into one blurred verdict.
Take from this month the case for spacing big efforts. Two rested pushes beat one long strain; remember that shape.
End the month by checking the seam: anything dropped between the two strong weeks is still on the floor. Pick it up first.
Rate the month by what its first week set in motion, and be honest about what coasted after. Both lessons are useful.
The month spent its best early; the close is for consolidation. Lock in what the fast start bought before it drifts.
As this month ends, resist calling the quiet finish a decline. The work was front-loaded by design — yours or the sky's.
Close by converting early momentum into standing structure: the habit, the agreement, the draft. Momentum unbanked evaporates.
What the opening week launched, the final weeks were for landing. If something never launched, note the timing cost — and forgive it.
The month ends on its strongest ground — finish accordingly. Do not let a late peak dissipate into tidying.
Close hard: the final week is the month's gift, and gifts unopened do not roll over.
If the month felt slow, check the ending before judging it. Late-carried months pay at the close, and this was one.
Bank the finish. What the last week delivered, write down, submit, or say out loud before the momentum resets.
Leave this month at full speed and let the next one inherit it. A strong close is a running start wearing a different name.
In review, the month's middle carried its meaning. Keep what the central week produced visible, so the slower edges do not rewrite the story.
The month peaked where months rarely get credit — the middle. Give the mid-point its due when you tally what happened.
Close the month by checking what descended from its central week: decisions, drafts, promises. That week was the source; trace its children.
A centre-carried month ends quietly by design. Quiet endings after loaded middles are completion, not fade.
Take the arch as the lesson: strong middles need honest ends. Close the remaining loose threads without demanding new heights of yourself.
